structural_claim: Symmetry breaking leads to emergent collective modes with properties fundamentally different from the underlying constituents

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Gauge bosons acquire mass through interaction with a field whose vacuum expectation value breaks the symmetry, creating massive collective excitations from massless constituents
Collective vibrations of atoms in a lattice create quasiparticle excitations (phonons) with properties distinct from individual atomic motion
Engineered structures create effective material properties impossible in natural materials through collective behavior of subwavelength elements
